author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Of the goodness of God explained in the creation of things, and of the Trinity as found in the first words of Genesis. The story concerning the origin of the world (Gen. I.) is allegorically explained, and he applies it to those things which God works for sanctified and blessed man. Finally, he makes an end of this work, having implored eternal rest from God.
He Recognises the Trinity in the First Two Verses of Genesis.

ccel.title: NPNF1-01. The Confessions and Letters of St. Augustine, with a Sketch of his Life and Work

source.title: NPNF1-01. The Confessions and Letters of St. Augustine, with a Sketch of his Life and Work

originalBook: Of the goodness of God explained in the creation of things, and of the Trinity as found in the first words of Genesis. The story concerning the origin of the world (Gen. I.) is allegorically explained, and he applies it to those things which God works for sanctified and blessed man. Finally, he makes an end of this work, having implored eternal rest from God.
title: He Recognises the Trinity in the First Two Verses of Genesis.

source.title: NPNF1-01. The Confessions and Letters of St. Augustine, with a Sketch of his Life and Work

verses.text: Behold now, the Trinity appears unto me in an enigma, which Thou, O my God, art, since Thou, O Father, in the Beginning of our wisdom,—Which is Thy Wisdom, born of Thyself, equal and co-eternal unto Thee,—that is, in Thy Son, hast created heaven and earth.

Many things have we said of the heaven of heavens, and of the earth invisible and formless, and of the darksome deep, in reference to the wandering defects of its spiritual deformity, were it not converted unto Him from whom was its life, such as it was, and by His enlightening became a beauteous life, and the heaven of that heaven which was afterwards set between water and water.

And under the name of God, I now held the Father, who made these things; and under the name of the Beginning, the Son, in whom He made these things; and believing, as I did, that my God was the Trinity, I sought further in His holy words, and behold, Thy Spirit was borne over the waters.
Behold the Trinity, O my God, Father, Son, and Holy Ghost,—the Creator of all creation.
